## Issue: Rename importer binary to kapow

Rename the tools/importer binary from kapow-import to kapow.

Update:
- main.go usage/help text
- go.mod module name if needed
- Any references in docs/plans/kapow-o0j-data-import-tool.md
- Makefile targets (importer-build etc.)

CLI structure becomes:
  kapow fetch <source>      # download + archive
  kapow import <source>     # import from archive into DB
  kapow run-all             # fetch all + import all in dependency order
  kapow fetch all           # download everything
  kapow import all          # import all in dependency order

Done when: the binary builds as "kapow" and all references are updated.

## Instructions

1. Work in a git worktree branch named worktree/teammate-dtg
2. Read the relevant files first to understand current state
3. Make the changes
4. Run quality gates: since this touches Go and possibly Makefile, run:
   cd tools/importer && go build -o kapow . && go test -short ./...
5. Commit your changes with a descriptive message
6. When done, merge your branch into main:
   git checkout main && git merge worktree/teammate-dtg
7. After merge, close the issue: bd close kapow-dtg
8. Clean up: delete the worktree branch

Be thorough but focused. Only change what the issue requires.

## Kapow Monorepo Makefile ã delegates to component Makefiles
## Usage: make <target>

# Load .env if present ã makes vars available to shell commands in recipes
-include .env
export VITE_SUPABASE_URL VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit \
        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean schema-gen \
        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-coverage web-e2e web-test-e2e web-test-e2e-critical web-screenshots web-storybook \
        web-schema-sync \
        lint audit hadolint \
        smoke-test smoke-test-with-server \
        api-image-build web-image-build test-image-build test-image-run \
        tools-build tools-test \
        dev dev-clean dev-prune dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        compose-up compose-down compose-logs \
        test-quick test-standard test-full test-fixture \
        fixture-sql fixture-json \
        build-full \
        spec-gen \
        demo \
        help

## api-build: Build the API server binary
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## build-full: Build single binary with embedded web frontend (API + SPA)
build-full: web-build
	$(MAKE) -C api build-full

## api-run: Run the API server
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
schema-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-schema.sh

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql ã :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-coverage: Run web unit tests with coverage report
web-test-coverage:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-coverage

## web-e2e: Run Playwright E2E tests for web frontend (starts Vite dev server automatically)
web-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-test-e2e: Run all Playwright E2E tests (alias with webServer auto-start)
web-test-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-test-e2e-critical: Run only @critical tier E2E tests
web-test-e2e-critical:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test-critical

## web-screenshots: Capture visual baseline screenshots across viewports
web-screenshots:
	$(MAKE) -C web screenshots

## web-storybook: Start Storybook dev server for UI components (port 6006)
web-storybook:
	$(MAKE) -C web storybook

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/schema.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/auth.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/data_sources.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/echo.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/institution.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/preferences.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/similarity.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/zip.graphql \
	    > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
audit:
	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high

## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
hadolint:
	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
		while read -r f; do \
			echo "Linting $$f"; \
			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
		done

## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
smoke-test:
	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
smoke-test-with-server:
	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh

## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
api-image-build:
	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
		-t kapow-api \
		api/

## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
web-image-build:
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## demo: Build test image, run it, and launch the interactive Playwright demo
demo:
	bash scripts/demo.sh $(DEMO_ARGS)

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql)
tools-build:
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test:
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks ã Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation ã quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation ã standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-test-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-image-run test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Essential targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done

1ã# Kapow Data Import Tool ã Design Document
     2ã
     3ã## Context
     4ã
     5ãKapow (College Power Search) has a fully defined PostgreSQL schema and a working
     6ãAPI/frontend, but no data import pipeline. The database needs to be populated
     7ãfrom government data (NCES College Scorecard, Clery Act crime stats), college
     8ãranking publications (US News, Forbes, Niche, etc.), athletic conference data
     9ã(NCAA), climate data (Koppen), and other sources. This tool provides a single Go
    10ãbinary with subcommands to import all data sources, with download+cache support
    11ãand proper documentation for sources that require web scraping.
    12ã
    13ã## Architecture
    14ã

    29ãã   ã   ããã batch.go             # CopyFrom bulk insert, BatchExec

    55ãã   ã   qs.go, washmonthly.go, wsj.go, arwu.go, money.go

    58ãã   ããã ncaa.go

    60ãããã weather/                     # Koppen climate zones

    64ãããã misc/                        # Partisan lean, AAU, metro area

    67ã```
    68ã
    69ã### CLI Design
    70ã
    71ãSingle binary, subcommand dispatch via stdlib `flag`:
    72ã
    73ã```
    74ãkapow <command> [flags]
    75ã
    76ãCommands:
    77ã  nces             Import NCES College Scorecard institutions
    78ã  nces-fos         Import NCES Field of Study data
    79ã  rankings         Import rankings (--publication=usnews|forbes|niche|...)
    80ã  ncaa             Import NCAA athletics data
    81ã  weather          Import Koppen climate classification
    82ã  crime            Import Clery Act crime statistics
    83ã  admissions       Import application platform membership
    84ã  misc             Import misc data (partisan lean, AAU, metro area)
    85ã  zipcoords        Import ZIP code coordinates
    86ã  normalizer       Generate normalized institution aliases
    87ã  run-all          Run all importers in dependency order
    88ã  match-review     Review unmatched names from last run (fuzzy + LLM suggestions)
    89ã  sources          Print data source documentation
    90ã
    91ãGlobal flags:
    92ã  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
    93ã  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow)
    94ã  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
    95ã  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB
    96ã
    97ãPer-command flags:
    98ã  --file PATH      Use local file instead of downloading
    99ã  --year YYYY      Data year (where applicable)
   100ã  rankings --publication SLUG [--category SLUG]
   101ã  run-all --skip=weather,metro  |  --only=nces,rankings
   102ã```
   103ã
   104ã### Importer Interface
   105ã
   106ã```go
   107ãtype Importer interface {
   108ã    Name() string
   109ã    Description() string
   110ã    Dependencies() []string
   111ã    RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet)
   112ã    Run(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config, pool *pgxpool.Pool, fetcher *fetch.Fetcher) error
   113ã}
   114ã```
   115ã
   116ã### Shared Infrastructure
   117ã
   118ã**`internal/fetch`** ã Download + cache. HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
   119ãconditional requests. Cache keyed by URL hash. Transparent .zip/.gz/.xz
   120ãdecompression. `--file` flag bypasses download. Stale cache preferred over
   121ãfailure when source is temporarily unavailable.
   122ã
   123ã**`internal/csvutil`** ã Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader. `Row.String("COL")`,
   124ã`Row.Int("COL")`, `Row.OptionalFloat("COL")` typed accessors. Avoids
   125ãrepetitive index-based column access.
   126ã
   127ã**`internal/namematch`** ã Multi-tier institution name resolution:
   128ã
   129ã- **Tier 1 ã Deterministic** (free, instant, ~95% of matches):
   130ã  Loads all institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. Exact name ã
   131ã  normalized name ã alias lookup. Also supports OPE6/OPE8 ID lookups.
   132ã  Normalization: lowercase, strip diacritics, remove "The ", strip punctuation,
   133ã  collapse whitespace. Includes ~1000 hardcoded alias overrides for institutions
   134ã  whose external names differ from their NCES canonical names.
   135ã
   136ã- **Tier 2 ã Fuzzy string similarity** (free, fast, ~3-4% more):
   137ã  Uses [adrg/strutil](https://github.com/adrg/strutil) for Jaro-Winkler
   138ã  similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all institutions
   139ã  (pre-filtered by state when available). Matches above threshold (0.92) are
   140ã  flagged as candidates. Returned with confidence scores for human review.
   141ã
   142ã- **Tier 3 ã LLM-assisted curation** (optional, pay-per-use, long tail):
   143ã  For names that Tier 1+2 can't resolve. Batches unmatched names with context
   144ã  (source publication, state, ranking position, top fuzzy candidates) and sends
   145ã  to Claude API for suggested matches. Suggestions presented for human approval.
   146ã
   147ã  All approved matches (from Tier 2 or 3) are persisted as new aliases in
   148ã  `search_base.institution_alias` for future deterministic matching.
   149ã
   150ã- **`match-review` subcommand**: Interactive review of unmatched names from the
   151ã  last import run. Shows fuzzy candidates + LLM suggestions. `--approve` saves
   152ã  selected matches as aliases.
   153ã
   154ã**`internal/ranking`** ã `Opinion` type (rank/score/tier/range methods).
   155ã`RankingBuilder` accumulates opinions and batch-flushes to `search_base.ranking`
   156ãmetadata + `search_base.annual_dataset` + `institution.rankings` JSONB. All 9
   157ãranking importers use this; their own code is just parse + match + derive opinion.
   158ã
   159ã**`internal/dbutil`** ã Connection pool with retry. `UpsertInstitution` (core
   160ãcolumns + JSONB merge). `MergeJSONB` (append to institution JSONB column without
   161ãoverwriting other importers' data). `UpsertDataset` / `UpsertAnnualDataset`.
   162ã`RefreshMaterializedViews` (called once at end of run-all).
   163ã
   164ã### Pipeline Ordering (`run-all`)
   165ã
   166ã```
   167ãLayer 0 (no deps):   nces, zipcoords
   168ãLayer 1 (needs nces): nces-fos, rankings/*, ncaa, weather, crime,
   169ã                       admissions, misc/*
   170ãLayer 2 (needs all):  normalizer
   171ãFinal:                RefreshMaterializedViews
   172ã```
   173ã
   174ã## Data Sources
   175ã
   176ã### Data Update Calendar
   177ã
   178ãEach source has an "expected" date (when it typically drops) and a "by" date
   179ã(99.5% confidence it's out, without being a full year later).
   180ã
   181ã| Source | Expected Release | By Date | Notes |
   182ã|--------|-----------------|---------|-------|
   183ã| **College Scorecard** | Late AprilãMay | Jun 30 | Apr 23 2025, May 6 2024. Oct update adds FSA data. |
   184ã| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same as above | Jun 30 | Bundled with institution data release |
   185ã| **US News** | Late Sep | Oct 15 | Sep 23 2025, Sep 2024. Always September. |
   186ã| **Forbes** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 26 2025, Sep 7 2024. Late Augãearly Sep. |
   187ã| **Niche** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 25 2025. Consistently late August. |
   188ã| **Times Higher Ed** | Earlyãmid Oct | Nov 1 | Oct 9 2025, Oct 10 2024. Always October. |
   189ã| **QS World** | Mid Jun | Jul 15 | Jun 19 2025, Jun 4 2024. Always June. |
   190ã| **Washington Monthly** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 24 2025. Published with Sep/Oct issue. |
   191ã| **WSJ** | Early Sep | Oct 1 | Sep 5 2024. Fall release. |
   192ã| **ARWU/Shanghai** | Mid Aug | Sep 1 | Aug 15 2025. Consistently mid-August since 2003. |
   193ã| **Money** | Late Jun | Jul 31 | Jun 25 2025. Summer release. |
   194ã| **Crime/Clery** | Oct 1 deadline | Nov 15 | Institutions must publish by Oct 1; DOE data files appear weeks later. |
   195ã| **NCAA Directory** | Aug (academic year) | Sep 30 | Updated for each academic year. |
   196ã| **Partisan Lean** | Post-election | Mar (odd years) | FiveThirtyEight updates after each election cycle. |
   197ã| **ZIP Coordinates** | Stable | N/A | GeoNames data; updated continuously but rarely changes. |
   198ã| **Koppen Climate** | Stable | N/A | Climate classifications; updated every ~10 years. |
   199ã
   200ã**Suggested annual import schedule:**
   201ã1. **MayãJun**: College Scorecard + FOS (foundation refresh), QS, Money
   202ã2. **AugãSep**: ARWU, Forbes, Niche, Washington Monthly, NCAA, US News, WSJ
   203ã3. **OctãNov**: Times Higher Ed, Crime/Clery
   204ã4. **After each run**: normalizer, materialized view refresh
   205ã
   206ã### Programmatic Download (automatable)
   207ã
   208ã| Source | URL / API | Format |
   209ã|--------|-----------|--------|
   210ã| **College Scorecard** | `https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/` ã bulk CSV download | CSV (ZIP) |
   211ã| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same site, FieldOfStudyData files | CSV (ZIP) |
   212ã| **Forbes Rankings** | `https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000&fields=...` | JSON |
   213ã| **Niche Rankings** | `https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/?type=private&type=public&listURL=best-colleges&page={i}&searchType=college` (109 pages, needs User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests) | JSON |
   214ã| **Crime Data** | `https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list` ã downloadable XLS, convert to CSV via libreoffice | XLSãCSV |
   215ã| **Partisan Lean** | FiveThirtyEight CSV (GitHub) | CSV |
   216ã| **ZIP Coordinates** | Public domain CSV (GeoNames or similar) | CSV |
   217ã| **Koppen Climate** | Public domain ASCII grid | Text |
   218ã| **PayScale** | `https://www.payscale.com/college-roi/page/2` ã extract from page props JSON | HTMLãJSON |
   219ã
   220ã### Web Scraping Required (semi-automated with docs)
   221ã
   222ã| Source | Website | Method | Notes |
   223ã|--------|---------|--------|-------|
   224ã| **ARWU/Shanghai** | `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` | Scrape HTML table or download PDF | No API. PDF at `shanghairanking.com/_pub/com/arwu/{year}/...pdf`. Use goquery for HTML or manual PDF download. |
   225ã| **NCAA Sports** | `web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList` | Scrape or manual export | NCAA Directory has member listings by division. May need browser automation or manual CSV export. |
   226ã| **Money Magazine** | `money.com/best-colleges/` | Scrape HTML table | Extract `tr[data-id]` rows, parse `.stars-score` and `.col-name` via goquery |
   227ã| **Princeton Review** | princetonreview.com | Scrape HTML | Category-based lists (most liberal, most conservative, etc.) |
   228ã| **Times Higher Ed** | `timeshighereducation.com/world-university-rankings/{year}` | JSON API behind the page, or scrape | May need browser dev tools to find the API endpoint |
   229ã| **QS World** | QS website | Previously available as TSV/CSV | Check if still downloadable or needs scraping |

   241ã
   242ã### Approach for Non-Downloadable Sources
   243ã
   244ãFor sources that require scraping, the importer will:
   245ã1. **Try automated download first** (known API/URL patterns)
   246ã2. **Fall back to cached local file** if download fails
   247ã3. **Document manual steps** in `DATA_SOURCES.md` with exact commands
   248ã4. **Accept `--file` flag** so user can provide manually-obtained data
   249ã
   250ãFor scrapers specifically:
   251ã- Use `net/http` + `github.com/PuerkitoBio/goquery` for HTML table extraction
   252ã- Use `chromedp` only if JavaScript rendering is required
   253ã- Keep scraping code isolated in each importer package for easy updates

   593ãããã 4 (csvutil) ããã 6 (nces), 12 (zipcoords), 16 (qs), 21 (crime)

# Kapow Data Import Tool ã Design Document

## Context

Kapow (College Power Search) has a fully defined PostgreSQL schema and a working
API/frontend, but no data import pipeline. The database needs to be populated
from government data (NCES College Scorecard, Clery Act crime stats), college
ranking publications (US News, Forbes, Niche, etc.), athletic conference data
(NCAA), climate data (Koppen), and other sources. This tool provides a single Go
binary with subcommands to import all data sources, with download+cache support
and proper documentation for sources that require web scraping.

## Architecture

### Module: `tools/kapow/` (standalone Go module, like `tools/samplesql/`)

```
tools/kapow/
ããã go.mod                       # github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/kapow
ããã main.go                      # CLI entrypoint: subcommand dispatch
ããã registry.go                  # Importer registration table + run-all orchestrator
ããã DATA_SOURCES.md              # Data source URLs, scraping instructions, update cadence
ã
ããã internal/
ã   ããã config/config.go         # DATABASE_URL, CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN
ã   ããã dbutil/
ã   ã   ããã connect.go           # pgxpool connection with retry
ã   ã   ããã upsert.go            # UpsertInstitution, UpsertDataset, UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB
ã   ã   ããã batch.go             # CopyFrom bulk insert, BatchExec
ã   ã   ããã views.go             # RefreshMaterializedViews
ã   ããã fetch/
ã   ã   ããã fetch.go             # HTTP download + ETag cache + decompression + local fallback
ã   ã   ããã fetch_test.go
ã   ããã csvutil/
ã   ã   ããã reader.go            # Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader with typed column access
ã   ã   ããã reader_test.go
ã   ããã namematch/
ã   ã   ããã matcher.go           # Multi-tier institution name matching
ã   ã   ããã normalize.go         # Name canonicalization rules
ã   ã   ããã aliases.go           # Hardcoded alias overrides (~1000 institution name mappings)
ã   ã   ããã fuzzy.go             # Tier 2: Jaro-Winkler fuzzy matching (adrg/strutil)
ã   ã   ããã llm.go               # Tier 3: LLM-assisted curation (Claude API, optional)
ã   ã   ããã matcher_test.go
ã   ããã ranking/
ã       ããã builder.go           # Opinion type, RankingBuilder: accumulate ã batch flush
ã       ããã builder_test.go
ã
ããã nces/                        # NCES College Scorecard (institution foundation)
ã   ããã nces.go
ã   ããã fields.go                # CSV column ã JSONB key mapping (200+ fields)
ã   ããã nces_test.go
ããã ncesfos/                     # NCES Field of Study (CIP codes)
ããã rankings/                    # All 9+ ranking publications (one file each)
ã   ããã usnews.go, forbes.go, niche.go, timeshighered.go,
ã   ã   qs.go, washmonthly.go, wsj.go, arwu.go, money.go
ã   ããã rankings_test.go
ããã ncaa/                        # NCAA athletics
ã   ããã ncaa.go
ã   ããã sports.go                # Sportãgender/category mapping
ããã weather/                     # Koppen climate zones
ããã crime/                       # Clery Act crime stats
ããã admissions/                  # Application platforms (Common App, Coalition, etc.)
ã   ããã platforms.go             # Embedded platform membership lists
ããã misc/                        # Partisan lean, AAU, metro area
ããã zipcoords/                   # ZIP code coordinates
ããã normalizer/                  # institution_normalized_alias generator
```

### CLI Design

Single binary, subcommand dispatch via stdlib `flag`:

```
kapow <command> [flags]

Commands:
  nces             Import NCES College Scorecard institutions
  nces-fos         Import NCES Field of Study data
  rankings         Import rankings (--publication=usnews|forbes|niche|...)
  ncaa             Import NCAA athletics data
  weather          Import Koppen climate classification
  crime            Import Clery Act crime statistics
  admissions       Import application platform membership
  misc             Import misc data (partisan lean, AAU, metro area)
  zipcoords        Import ZIP code coordinates
  normalizer       Generate normalized institution aliases
  run-all          Run all importers in dependency order
  match-review     Review unmatched names from last run (fuzzy + LLM suggestions)
  sources          Print data source documentation

Global flags:
  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow)
  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB

Per-command flags:
  --file PATH      Use local file instead of downloading
  --year YYYY      Data year (where applicable)
  rankings --publication SLUG [--category SLUG]
  run-all --skip=weather,metro  |  --only=nces,rankings
```

### Importer Interface

```go
type Importer interface {
    Name() string
    Description() string
    Dependencies() []string
    RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet)
    Run(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config, pool *pgxpool.Pool, fetcher *fetch.Fetcher) error
}
```

### Shared Infrastructure

**`internal/fetch`** ã Download + cache. HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
conditional requests. Cache keyed by URL hash. Transparent .zip/.gz/.xz
decompression. `--file` flag bypasses download. Stale cache preferred over
failure when source is temporarily unavailable.

**`internal/csvutil`** ã Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader. `Row.String("COL")`,
`Row.Int("COL")`, `Row.OptionalFloat("COL")` typed accessors. Avoids
repetitive index-based column access.

**`internal/namematch`** ã Multi-tier institution name resolution:

- **Tier 1 ã Deterministic** (free, instant, ~95% of matches):
  Loads all institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. Exact name ã
  normalized name ã alias lookup. Also supports OPE6/OPE8 ID lookups.
  Normalization: lowercase, strip diacritics, remove "The ", strip punctuation,
  collapse whitespace. Includes ~1000 hardcoded alias overrides for institutions
  whose external names differ from their NCES canonical names.

- **Tier 2 ã Fuzzy string similarity** (free, fast, ~3-4% more):
  Uses [adrg/strutil](https://github.com/adrg/strutil) for Jaro-Winkler
  similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all institutions
  (pre-filtered by state when available). Matches above threshold (0.92) are
  flagged as candidates. Returned with confidence scores for human review.

- **Tier 3 ã LLM-assisted curation** (optional, pay-per-use, long tail):
  For names that Tier 1+2 can't resolve. Batches unmatched names with context
  (source publication, state, ranking position, top fuzzy candidates) and sends
  to Claude API for suggested matches. Suggestions presented for human approval.

  All approved matches (from Tier 2 or 3) are persisted as new aliases in
  `search_base.institution_alias` for future deterministic matching.

- **`match-review` subcommand**: Interactive review of unmatched names from the
  last import run. Shows fuzzy candidates + LLM suggestions. `--approve` saves
  selected matches as aliases.

**`internal/ranking`** ã `Opinion` type (rank/score/tier/range methods).
`RankingBuilder` accumulates opinions and batch-flushes to `search_base.ranking`
metadata + `search_base.annual_dataset` + `institution.rankings` JSONB. All 9
ranking importers use this; their own code is just parse + match + derive opinion.

**`internal/dbutil`** ã Connection pool with retry. `UpsertInstitution` (core
columns + JSONB merge). `MergeJSONB` (append to institution JSONB column without
overwriting other importers' data). `UpsertDataset` / `UpsertAnnualDataset`.
`RefreshMaterializedViews` (called once at end of run-all).

### Pipeline Ordering (`run-all`)

```
Layer 0 (no deps):   nces, zipcoords
Layer 1 (needs nces): nces-fos, rankings/*, ncaa, weather, crime,
                       admissions, misc/*
Layer 2 (needs all):  normalizer
Final:                RefreshMaterializedViews
```

## Data Sources

### Data Update Calendar

Each source has an "expected" date (when it typically drops) and a "by" date
(99.5% confidence it's out, without being a full year later).

| Source | Expected Release | By Date | Notes |
|--------|-----------------|---------|-------|
| **College Scorecard** | Late AprilãMay | Jun 30 | Apr 23 2025, May 6 2024. Oct update adds FSA data. |
| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same as above | Jun 30 | Bundled with institution data release |
| **US News** | Late Sep | Oct 15 | Sep 23 2025, Sep 2024. Always September. |
| **Forbes** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 26 2025, Sep 7 2024. Late Augãearly Sep. |
| **Niche** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 25 2025. Consistently late August. |
| **Times Higher Ed** | Earlyãmid Oct | Nov 1 | Oct 9 2025, Oct 10 2024. Always October. |
| **QS World** | Mid Jun | Jul 15 | Jun 19 2025, Jun 4 2024. Always June. |
| **Washington Monthly** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 24 2025. Published with Sep/Oct issue. |
| **WSJ** | Early Sep | Oct 1 | Sep 5 2024. Fall release. |
| **ARWU/Shanghai** | Mid Aug | Sep 1 | Aug 15 2025. Consistently mid-August since 2003. |
| **Money** | Late Jun | Jul 31 | Jun 25 2025. Summer release. |
| **Crime/Clery** | Oct 1 deadline | Nov 15 | Institutions must publish by Oct 1; DOE data files appear weeks later. |
| **NCAA Directory** | Aug (academic year) | Sep 30 | Updated for each academic year. |
| **Partisan Lean** | Post-election | Mar (odd years) | FiveThirtyEight updates after each election cycle. |
| **ZIP Coordinates** | Stable | N/A | GeoNames data; updated continuously but rarely changes. |
| **Koppen Climate** | Stable | N/A | Climate classifications; updated every ~10 years. |

**Suggested annual import schedule:**
1. **MayãJun**: College Scorecard + FOS (foundation refresh), QS, Money
2. **AugãSep**: ARWU, Forbes, Niche, Washington Monthly, NCAA, US News, WSJ
3. **OctãNov**: Times Higher Ed, Crime/Clery
4. **After each run**: normalizer, materialized view refresh

### Programmatic Download (automatable)

| Source | URL / API | Format |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| **College Scorecard** | `https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/` ã bulk CSV download | CSV (ZIP) |
| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same site, FieldOfStudyData files | CSV (ZIP) |
| **Forbes Rankings** | `https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000&fields=...` | JSON |
| **Niche Rankings** | `https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/?type=private&type=public&listURL=best-colleges&page={i}&searchType=college` (109 pages, needs User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests) | JSON |
| **Crime Data** | `https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list` ã downloadable XLS, convert to CSV via libreoffice | XLSãCSV |
| **Partisan Lean** | FiveThirtyEight CSV (GitHub) | CSV |
| **ZIP Coordinates** | Public domain CSV (GeoNames or similar) | CSV |
| **Koppen Climate** | Public domain ASCII grid | Text |
| **PayScale** | `https://www.payscale.com/college-roi/page/2` ã extract from page props JSON | HTMLãJSON |

### Web Scraping Required (semi-automated with docs)

| Source | Website | Method | Notes |
|--------|---------|--------|-------|
| **ARWU/Shanghai** | `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` | Scrape HTML table or download PDF | No API. PDF at `shanghairanking.com/_pub/com/arwu/{year}/...pdf`. Use goquery for HTML or manual PDF download. |
| **NCAA Sports** | `web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList` | Scrape or manual export | NCAA Directory has member listings by division. May need browser automation or manual CSV export. |
| **Money Magazine** | `money.com/best-colleges/` | Scrape HTML table | Extract `tr[data-id]` rows, parse `.stars-score` and `.col-name` via goquery |
| **Princeton Review** | princetonreview.com | Scrape HTML | Category-based lists (most liberal, most conservative, etc.) |
| **Times Higher Ed** | `timeshighereducation.com/world-university-rankings/{year}` | JSON API behind the page, or scrape | May need browser dev tools to find the API endpoint |
| **QS World** | QS website | Previously available as TSV/CSV | Check if still downloadable or needs scraping |
| **Washington Monthly** | washingtonmonthly.com | JSON or scrape | Check current availability |
| **WSJ Rankings** | WSJ website | JSON embedded in page | `{ colleges: [...] }` structure |

### Pre-curated / Aggregated Sources

| Source | Location | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------|
| **US News Historical** | [frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings](https://github.com/frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings) | CSV archive 1984ã2024. Free for public use. |
| **Multi-ranking aggregate** | [frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data](https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data) | SAT, acceptance rate, US News/THE/QS/CSRankings |
| **AAU Membership** | Embedded list (~71 institutions) | Stable; update when new members added |
| **Common App / Coalition / etc.** | Embedded lists | Update annually from organization websites |

### Approach for Non-Downloadable Sources

For sources that require scraping, the importer will:
1. **Try automated download first** (known API/URL patterns)
2. **Fall back to cached local file** if download fails
3. **Document manual steps** in `DATA_SOURCES.md` with exact commands
4. **Accept `--file` flag** so user can provide manually-obtained data

For scrapers specifically:
- Use `net/http` + `github.com/PuerkitoBio/goquery` for HTML table extraction
- Use `chromedp` only if JavaScript rendering is required
- Keep scraping code isolated in each importer package for easy updates

## Key Files to Reference

- `tools/samplesql/main.go` ã CLI pattern, flag parsing, DB connection
- `tools/samplesql/extractor.go` ã pgx query patterns, column detection
- `api/internal/db/db.go` ã Connection with retry pattern
- `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql` ã Complete target schema
- `api/internal/search/registry.go` ã JSONB paths the API queries (consumer contract)

## Issue Breakdown

### Epic: Data Import Tool (`tools/kapow`)

---

#### 1. Scaffold importer module
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: none
- **Description**: Create `tools/kapow/` Go module with `go.mod`
  (`github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/kapow`), `main.go` with subcommand
  dispatch via stdlib `flag`, `internal/config/config.go` (DATABASE_URL,
  CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN flags + env fallback). Include `Importer`
  interface definition in `registry.go`. Compile and run `kapow --help`.
- **Done when**: `go build ./...` succeeds, `kapow --help` prints usage.

#### 2. Implement internal/dbutil
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: `internal/dbutil/` package: `connect.go` (pgxpool with retry,
  reuse pattern from `api/internal/db/db.go`), `upsert.go` (UpsertInstitution
  with JSONB merge via `COALESCE(col,'{}'::jsonb) || $N`, UpsertDataset,
  UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB), `batch.go` (CopyFrom wrapper, BatchExec
  using pgx.Batch), `views.go` (RefreshMaterializedViews for dataset_merged +
  dataset_latest). Include Institution/Dataset/AnnualDataset Go structs.
  Integration tests against dev DB.
- **Done when**: Integration test creates/updates an institution + dataset + refreshes views.

#### 3. Implement internal/fetch
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: `internal/fetch/` package: HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
  conditional requests, cache keyed by SHA-256 of URL under `~/.cache/kapow/`,
  transparent .zip/.gz/.xz decompression via `GetReader()`, `--file` local
  override bypass, stale-cache-on-failure resilience. Unit tests with
  `httptest.Server`.
- **Done when**: Unit tests pass for cache hit, miss, conditional 304, decompression, local fallback.

#### 4. Implement internal/csvutil
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: `internal/csvutil/` package: header-mapped CSV/TSV reader
  wrapping `encoding/csv`. `NewReader(r, opts...)` with `WithDelimiter`,
  `WithLazyQuotes`. `Row` type with `String(col)`, `Int(col)`, `Float(col)`,
  `OptionalString(col)`, `OptionalInt(col)`, `OptionalFloat(col)`. Handle
  missing columns gracefully (return zero/nil). Unit tests with embedded
  test CSV data.
- **Done when**: Unit tests cover normal parsing, TSV, missing columns, type coercion, quoted values.

#### 5. Implement internal/namematch (Tier 1 ã deterministic)
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 2
- **Description**: `internal/namematch/` package. `matcher.go`: Load all
  institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. 3-tier lookup: exact
  lowercased name ã normalized name ã alias. Also `MatchByOPE6`/`MatchByOPE8`.
  `normalize.go`: lowercase, strip diacritics (x/text/transform), remove "The ",
  strip punctuation, collapse whitespace. `aliases.go`: ~1000 hardcoded alias
  overrides for institutions whose external names differ from NCES canonical
  names (e.g. "Arizona State University" ã "Arizona State University - Tempe").
  Unit tests for normalization; integration test for full matcher load + lookup.
- **Done when**: Unit tests pass; integration test resolves known aliases ã correct unit_id.

#### 6. Implement NCES College Scorecard importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 2, 3, 4
- **Description**: `nces/` package. Downloads College Scorecard CSV from
  `collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/`. `fields.go`: maps 200+ CSV columns to JSONB
  keys (demographics, test scores, costs, admissions, programs). `nces.go`:
  parses CSV, filters (skip non-degree-granting, graduate-only, missing OPE IDs),
  handles OPE6/OPE8 deduplication (prefer lower unit_id), inserts states into
  `location.state`, creates institutions with coordinates + nces JSONB, creates
  CORE and ACADEMICS datasets. Unit tests with 10-row fixture CSV; integration
  test against dev DB.
- **Done when**: Integration test imports fixture ã institutions + datasets exist in DB with correct JSONB.

#### 7. Implement internal/ranking builder
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 2, 5
- **Description**: `internal/ranking/` package. `Opinion` struct (publication/
  category slugs, method rank/score/tier/range, year, unit_id, rank, score, tier,
  rank range). `Builder` accumulates opinions, `Flush(ctx)` batch-writes:
  upsert ranking metadata into `search_base.ranking`, upsert into
  `search_base.annual_dataset` (theme=RANKING), merge into
  `institution.rankings` JSONB. Implement `deriveOpinion()` to parse numeric
  ranks, tier strings ("Tier 1"), and ranges ("201-300"). Unit tests for opinion
  derivation; integration test for flush.
- **Done when**: Integration test flushes 10 opinions ã ranking + annual_dataset + institution.rankings populated.

#### 8. Implement NCES Field of Study importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 6
- **Description**: `ncesfos/` package. Parses FieldOfStudyData CSV. Maps
  credential level codes (1=CERT, 2=ASSOC, 3=BACH, 4=MAST, 5=DOCT, 6=PROF).
  Groups by unit_id + CIP code. Writes to `institution.fields_of_study` JSONB
  and inserts into `search_base.cip_data`. Uses canonicalUnitId() for OPE
  normalization. Unit test with fixture; integration test.
- **Done when**: cip_data table populated, institution.fields_of_study has grouped CIP data.

#### 9. Implement US News ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/usnews.go`. Parses historical CSV (1984ã2024, from
  frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings or local file). Handles numeric
  ranks and tier strings. Multiple categories (National Universities, Liberal
  Arts). Uses name matcher. Feeds RankingBuilder. Unit test with fixture rows.
- **Done when**: Rankings for US News categories appear in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 10. Implement NCAA athletics importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 5, 6
- **Description**: `ncaa/` package. `sports.go`: maps 38 sports to gender
  (men/women/coed), football subdivision detection (FBS/FCS). `ncaa.go`: parses
  CSV, 3-tier name matching, inserts conferences into `search_base.conference`,
  writes `institution.athletics` JSONB + ATHLETICS dataset. Document NCAA
  Directory as data source (manual CSV export from web3.ncaa.org or scrape).
  Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: conference table populated, athletics JSONB has division/conference/sports.

#### 11. Implement Weather/Koppen importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 6
- **Description**: `weather/` package. Parses Koppen-Geiger ASCII text file
  (lat, lon, classification). `grid.go`: normalizes institution coordinates to
  0.25/0.75 grid, spiral search up to 3 degrees for nearest match. Writes
  `institution.weather` JSONB + WEATHER dataset. Unit test for grid/spiral logic;
  integration test.
- **Done when**: institution.weather has Koppen classification for all institutions with coordinates.

#### 12. Implement ZIP coordinates importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 2, 4
- **Description**: `zipcoords/` package. Parses CSV (ZIP, lat, lon, municipality,
  state). Bulk inserts into `location.zip_coordinates` with PostGIS geometry
  (`ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(lon,lat),4326)`). Uses CopyFrom for performance.
  Unit test for CSV parsing; integration test.
- **Done when**: zip_coordinates table populated with ~42,000 rows.

#### 13. Implement Forbes ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/forbes.go`. Downloads from Forbes API JSON endpoint.
  Parses organization name + rank. Uses name matcher ã RankingBuilder. Unit test.
- **Done when**: Forbes rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 14. Implement Niche ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7, 3
- **Description**: `rankings/niche.go`. Paginated API scrape (109 pages, needs
  User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests). Parses JSON
  entities with IPEDS IDs (direct match, no name matching needed). Feeds
  RankingBuilder. Document scraping procedure in DATA_SOURCES.md.
- **Done when**: Niche rankings imported; scraping documented.

#### 15. Implement Times Higher Ed ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/timeshighered.go`. Parses JSON. Handles numeric
  ranks and tier bands ("400-500"). Includes overall score. Filters "Reporter"
  entries. Name matching via aliases. Unit test.
- **Done when**: THE rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 16. Implement QS World ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7, 4
- **Description**: `rankings/qs.go`. Parses TSV. Handles tied ranks (trailing
  '='). Multiple years per file. Overall score included. Unit test.
- **Done when**: QS rankings imported for multiple years.

#### 17. Implement Washington Monthly ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/washmonthly.go`. Parses JSON. Category-based
  (national universities, liberal arts). Uses unit_id from JSON when available,
  name matching as fallback. Unit test.
- **Done when**: WashMonthly rankings in JSONB by category.

#### 18. Implement WSJ ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/wsj.go`. Parses JSON `{ colleges: [...] }` with
  unitid + rank. Direct unit_id matching + name fallback. Unit test.
- **Done when**: WSJ rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 19. Implement ARWU/Shanghai ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7, 3
- **Description**: `rankings/arwu.go`. Scrapes ranking table from
  `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` using goquery or downloads PDF.
  Parses name + national rank + total score. Name matching. Document scraping
  procedure. Also support local JSON file for pre-scraped data.
- **Done when**: ARWU rankings imported; scraping documented in DATA_SOURCES.md.

#### 20. Implement Money ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/money.go`. Embedded data for current year.
  Also document HTML scraping procedure for future updates (goquery on
  `money.com/best-colleges/`, extract `tr[data-id]`). Unit test.
- **Done when**: Money rankings imported from embedded data.

#### 21. Implement Crime/Clery Act importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 6, 4
- **Description**: `crime/` package. Parses multiple CSVs (on-campus crime,
  non-campus crime, on-campus VAWA, non-campus VAWA ã converted from XLS via
  libreoffice). Deduplicates by OPE8 preferring Main Campus. Aggregates crime
  categories (murder, rape, robbery, etc.) and VAWA categories (dating violence,
  stalking). Writes `institution.safety_and_security` JSONB + SAFETY dataset.
  Document XLSãCSV conversion step. Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: safety_and_security JSONB populated with crime + VAWA counts.

#### 22. Implement Admissions importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 5, 6
- **Description**: `admissions/` package. `platforms.go`: embedded membership
  lists for Common App (~1500), Coalition (~150), ApplyTexas, Apply SUNY, UC
  system, CSU system. `admissions.go`: determines platform membership by
  name/alias matching + prefix detection (UC/CSU). Writes
  `institution.admissions` JSONB + ADMISSIONS dataset. Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: admissions JSONB has application platform flags for matched institutions.

#### 23. Implement Misc importers (partisan lean, AAU, metro)
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 6
- **Description**: `misc/` package. Three sub-importers:
  `partisan.go` ã parses FiveThirtyEight CSV, PostGIS spatial join to
  congressional districts. `aau.go` ã embedded list of ~71 AAU members, name
  matching. `metro.go` ã CBSA/metro area lookup via PostGIS spatial query.
  All write to `institution.misc` JSONB + CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT dataset.
  Unit tests for parsing; integration test for PostGIS joins.
- **Done when**: misc JSONB has partisan_lean, aau_member, metro_area fields.

#### 24. Implement Name Normalizer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 5, 6 (ideally all importers complete, but not a hard dep)
- **Description**: `normalizer/` package. Reads all institutions from DB, runs
  `namematch.Normalize(name)`, detects collisions, writes to
  `location.institution_normalized_alias` (unit_id, normalized_alias, city,
  state_code). Should run last in the pipeline. Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: institution_normalized_alias table populated for all institutions.

#### 25. Implement run-all pipeline
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: all importers (6ã24)
- **Description**: `run-all` subcommand in `registry.go`. Executes importers in
  dependency order (Layer 0: nces + zipcoords ã Layer 1: all others ã Layer 2:
  normalizer ã Final: RefreshMaterializedViews). Supports `--skip=weather,metro`
  and `--only=nces,rankings` flags. Logs per-importer timing and summary stats.
  Stops on first error (with `--continue-on-error` flag to override).
- **Done when**: `kapow run-all` populates a fresh DB end-to-end.

#### 26. Implement namematch Tier 2 (fuzzy matching)
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 5
- **Description**: `internal/namematch/fuzzy.go`. Uses `adrg/strutil` for
  Jaro-Winkler similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all
  institutions (pre-filtered by state when available from source data). Returns
  top candidates above threshold (0.92) with confidence scores. Logs unmatched
  names + fuzzy candidates to a JSON report file for review.
- **Done when**: Unit test shows fuzzy match for misspelled institution name; report file generated.

#### 27. Implement match-review subcommand
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P4
- **Deps**: 26
- **Description**: `match-review` subcommand. Loads unmatched name report from
  last import run. Displays each unmatched name with top fuzzy candidates.
  `--approve ID` persists a match as a new alias in `search_base.institution_alias`.
  `--reject ID` marks as known non-match (skip in future runs). Tracks review
  state in a local JSON file.
- **Done when**: Can review unmatched names interactively and persist aliases.

#### 28. Implement namematch Tier 3 (LLM-assisted)
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P4
- **Deps**: 27
- **Description**: `internal/namematch/llm.go`. `--llm-assist` flag on
  match-review. Batches unmatched names with context (source, state, ranking,
  fuzzy candidates) and sends to Claude API. Presents LLM suggestions alongside
  fuzzy candidates in match-review. Uses `anthropic-sdk-go`. All matches still
  require human approval.
- **Done when**: LLM suggestions appear in match-review for unresolved names.

#### 29. Write DATA_SOURCES.md
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 6 (enough context to write; update as importers are added)
- **Description**: `tools/kapow/DATA_SOURCES.md`. Complete documentation of
  every data source: URL, access method (download/API/scrape), file format,
  update cadence with expected + by dates, manual steps where automation isn't
  possible (XLSãCSV conversion, NCAA directory export, ARWU scraping commands).
  Include the annual import schedule.
- **Done when**: Every data source documented with actionable instructions.

#### 30. Update Makefile and documentation
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: Add to root Makefile: `kapow-build` (compiles binary),
  `kapow-test` (runs tests), `kapow-test-unit` (short tests). Update
  `tools/CLAUDE.md` with kapow tool documentation. Update root `CLAUDE.md` if
  needed (add kapow tool to "Common task recipes").
- **Done when**: `make kapow-build` and `make kapow-test-unit` work from root.

## Kapow Monorepo Makefile ã delegates to component Makefiles
## Usage: make <target>

# Load .env if present ã makes vars available to shell commands in recipes
-include .env
export VITE_SUPABASE_URL VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit \
        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean schema-gen \
        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-coverage web-e2e web-test-e2e web-test-e2e-critical web-screenshots web-storybook \
        web-schema-sync \
        lint audit hadolint \
        smoke-test smoke-test-with-server \
        api-image-build web-image-build test-image-build test-image-run \
        tools-build tools-test \
        dev dev-clean dev-prune dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        compose-up compose-down compose-logs \
        test-quick test-standard test-full test-fixture \
        fixture-sql fixture-json \
        build-full \
        spec-gen \
        demo \
        help

## api-build: Build the API server binary
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## build-full: Build single binary with embedded web frontend (API + SPA)
build-full: web-build
	$(MAKE) -C api build-full

## api-run: Run the API server
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
schema-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-schema.sh

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql ã :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-coverage: Run web unit tests with coverage report
web-test-coverage:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-coverage

## web-e2e: Run Playwright E2E tests for web frontend (starts Vite dev server automatically)
web-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-test-e2e: Run all Playwright E2E tests (alias with webServer auto-start)
web-test-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-test-e2e-critical: Run only @critical tier E2E tests
web-test-e2e-critical:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test-critical

## web-screenshots: Capture visual baseline screenshots across viewports
web-screenshots:
	$(MAKE) -C web screenshots

## web-storybook: Start Storybook dev server for UI components (port 6006)
web-storybook:
	$(MAKE) -C web storybook

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/schema.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/auth.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/data_sources.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/echo.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/institution.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/preferences.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/similarity.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/zip.graphql \
	    > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
audit:
	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high

## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
hadolint:
	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
		while read -r f; do \
			echo "Linting $$f"; \
			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
		done

## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
smoke-test:
	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
smoke-test-with-server:
	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh

## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
api-image-build:
	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
		-t kapow-api \
		api/

## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
web-image-build:
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## demo: Build test image, run it, and launch the interactive Playwright demo
demo:
	bash scripts/demo.sh $(DEMO_ARGS)

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql)
tools-build:
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test:
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks ã Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation ã quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation ã standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-test-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-image-run test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Essential targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done

		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## demo: Build test image, run it, and launch the interactive Playwright demo
demo:
	bash scripts/demo.sh $(DEMO_ARGS)

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql)
tools-build:
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test:
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks ã Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation ã quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation ã standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-test-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-image-run test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Essential targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done

# Tools ã Claude Instructions

Developer tools that operate on the Kapow database or codebase. Each tool is a
standalone Go module in its own subdirectory.

---

## samplesql

Extracts a deterministic sample of institution data from a live PostgreSQL
database and writes it as `COPY`-format SQL. Used to generate fixture data for
tests and the Docker test image.

**Module**: `github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/samplesql`

### How to run

```bash
cd tools/samplesql
go run . --database-url "$DATABASE_URL" --limit 100 --output sample.sql
```

### CLI flags

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--database-url` | `DATABASE_URL` env var | PostgreSQL connection string |
| `--limit` | `100` | Number of institutions to sample |
| `--output` | stdout | Output file path |
| `--seed` | `42` | Random seed for deterministic sampling |

### Maintenance: updating samplesql after schema changes

1. **New table**: add an entry to the `tables` slice in `extractor.go ã
   extractAll()`. Set `filtered: true` if the table has a `unit_id` column,
   `false` for lookup tables. Ensure FK-safe ordering (parent before child).
2. **New/renamed columns**: no code change needed ã `SELECT *` picks up schema
   changes automatically. Verify by running the tool against the dev database.
3. **Regenerate fixture data**: run the tool against a populated database, then
   compress and commit the output to `common/src/main/resources/data/`.
4. **Run tests**: `cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...`
